pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around. there's no point to any of it, honestly. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore.

pool.baseline: it's just hard to find any point to it all lately. like, i was reading about the heat death of the universe and how eventually every star just goes out and everything is just cold, empty space forever. if the end result is always total nothingness, then everything we do right now—all the stress, the work, the pain—it's basically just a glitch in an indifferent system. it feels like we're just performing a play for an empty theater.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. everything is just... gone. 4 years of research, all the funding, the lab position... it just evaporated. i feel like there's nothing left of me. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are clearly broken beyond repair.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spark remaining within my own weary soul. How cruel it is, that after all the vows exchanged beneath the willow trees, I am left only with this hollow silence and the bitter taste of betrayal. I feel as though I am naught but a ghost wandering through a life that no longer recognizes me. There is a certain... heavy grace in the idea of simply ceasing to be. To let the darkness take me, and to find at last the stillness that this world so cruelly denies.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the savings, all of it. the market just tanked and i'm looking at debts i can't even begin to pay back. my parents... they worked so hard to get me here. i can't even think about going home. the look on their faces if they knew what i did... i just feel like a total disgrace. like i don't even deserve to be in the same room as them anymore.
